title: Communauté d'agglomération Flers Agglo
summary: Communauté d'agglomération Flers Agglo is the communauté d'agglomération, an intercommunal structure, centred on the town of Flers. It is located in the Orne department, in the Normandy region, northwestern France. Created in 2013, its seat is in Flers. Its area is 567.7 km2. Its population was 53,555 in 2019, of which 14,762 in Flers proper. It covers some of the Communes that make up the area known as Suisse Normande.
text:
Communauté d'agglomération Flers Agglo is the communauté d'agglomération, an intercommunal structure, centred on the town of Flers. It is located in the Orne department, in the Normandy region, northwestern France. Created in 2013, its seat is in Flers. Its area is 567.7 km2. Its population was 53,555 in 2019, of which 14,762 in Flers proper. It covers some of the Communes that make up the area known as Suisse Normande.

Composition
The communauté d'agglomération consists of the following 42 communes:
